verses.text: David pleads that God would not rebuke him in anger but have mercy, for he is weak and his very bones are troubled. He feels chastening as a heavy hand. The believer under the rod cries not for justice but for tender mercy.

David pleads that God would not rebuke him in anger but have mercy, for he is weak and his very bones are troubled. He feels chastening as a heavy hand. The believer under the rod cries not for justice but for tender mercy.

David pleads that God would not rebuke him in anger but have mercy, for he is weak and his very bones are troubled. He feels chastening as a heavy hand. The believer under the rod cries not for justice but for tender mercy.
'Return, O LORD, deliver my soul,' he prays, for in death there is no remembrance of God. He longs to live to praise Him. Life is precious chiefly as a time to know and serve the Lord.
'Return, O LORD, deliver my soul,' he prays, for in death there is no remembrance of God. He longs to live to praise Him. Life is precious chiefly as a time to know and serve the Lord.
Worn out with groaning, he drenches his bed with tears all night long. His grief is deep and real. God does not despise the tears of His suffering child, but counts them every one.
Worn out with groaning, he drenches his bed with tears all night long. His grief is deep and real. God does not despise the tears of His suffering child, but counts them every one.

Then faith breaks through: 'depart from me... the LORD hath heard the voice of my weeping,' and his enemies shall be ashamed. Prayer turns sorrow to assurance. The same God who let him weep now answers, and the tide of fear is turned.
